title: mandevilla urceolata
intro: Mandevilla urceolata, also known as the white mandevilla, is a type of flowering plant native to South America, characterized by its white, funnel-shaped flowers and climbing habit. Traditionally, Mandevilla urceolata has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting oxidative stress, and exhibiting antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, Mandevilla urceolata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its extracts are also used in pharmaceutical and cosmetic applications.
